name: zencode
description: A wellness-focused coding companion that tracks cognitive load, provides wellness insights, and promotes healthy coding habits.

zencode.fatigueThreshold.description: Fatigue score percentage above which warnings appear.

zencode.strictMode.description: Show modal warnings when fatigue is detected.

zencode.autoZenMode.description: Automatically enable VS Code Zen Mode once per fatigue episode.

zencode.postureReminderInterval.description: Posture reminder interval in minutes. Set to 0 to disable.

zencode.smartBreakDuration.description: Continuous active coding duration in minutes before suggesting a break.

zencode.wellnessNotifications.description: Enable wellness notifications such as posture, hydration, eye, and break reminders.

zencode.hydrationInterval.description: Hydration reminder interval in minutes. Set to 0 to disable.

zencode.eyeExerciseInterval.description: Eye exercise reminder interval in minutes. Set to 0 to disable.

zencode.samplingWindowMinutes.description: Rolling time window used to calculate fatigue signals.

zencode.minimumKeystrokeThreshold.description: Minimum recent keystrokes required before ZenCode shows a fatigue score.

zencode.excludedLanguages.description: Language IDs where ZenCode should not monitor typing.

zencode.excludedWorkspaces.description: Workspace path fragments where ZenCode should not monitor typing.

zencode.dataRetentionDays.description: Number of days to keep local fatigue history.

zencode.statusBarPosition.description: Status bar side used by ZenCode.

zencode.notificationStyle.description: How loudly ZenCode should notify when fatigue is detected.

zencode.workdayStartHour.description: Hour when ZenCode should start monitoring. Same start and end means all day.

zencode.workdayEndHour.description: Hour when ZenCode should stop monitoring. Same start and end means all day.

zencode.privacyMode.description: Keep wellness data in memory only and skip local history persistence.

scripts.test: npm run compile && node out/test/fatigueDetector.test.js && node out/test/utils.test.js
